title: Use yaml file's parent  as the working directory for `podman kube play`
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17177
Originally posted by **abalmos** January 20, 2023 I was hoping to use `podman kube play` and the systemd `podman-kube@.service` to replace existing docker-compose setups. Generally, we put all needed resources (config files, data volume mounts, etc.) into one folder. Then, with compose, one can refer to them using relative paths from the YAML file. As a result, the parent folder is somewhat portable--in the sense that one can move it and `docker-compose up` still does the right thing. With `podman kube play` relative paths work, but they are relative to `podman`'s current working directory, not the location of the yaml file being played. This renders `podman-kube@.service` hard to use because the current working directory is not the parent folder of the yaml file being started. Therefore, all paths need to made absolute and now the "service folder" must know about where it lives on a particular machine. Is it currently possible to make `podman` use the parent directory of the yaml as the current working directory? If not, perhaps that could at least be a cli flag, if not the default behavior?

comments.body: Can you outline exactly what needs to be done and how it should behave?  I am not sure whether I interpret it correctly and want to make sure we're not closing doors for future features.  For instance, I crossed an idea of allowing more than one K8s YAML file as input.  That would be ambiguous in a way.  Maybe we need a root flag such as `podman --workdir`?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
